text:
I suspect the truth is that we are waiting,
all of us,
against insurmountable odds,
for something extraordinary to happen to us.
author: N/A

text:
In May, anything seemed possible.
If only I could learn to harness time itself.
author: N/A

text: Life covered by a veil of melancholy.
author: N/A

text:
it's April, no May, it's May
Such little things have to be established in morning
after the big things of night
author: N/A

text:
In the deepening spring of
May, I had no choice but to
recognize the trembling of
my heart.
author: N/A

text:
And so I am inclined to think
the best and most sensible
solution all round would be for
me to go away and to keep my
distance, to cease to be, as it were.
author: N/A
resources: Vincent van Gogh, from a letter to Theo van Gogh, written July 1880; The Letters of Vincent van Gogh

text:
He was my muse. My notepad is full of writing inspired by his being; that writer love was temporarily impeccable.
If I didn't let my heart love him I wouldn't have pages of greatness to revisit.
author: N/A

text:
Your memory is a monster; you
forget—it doesn't. It simply files
things away. It keeps things for you,
or hides things from you—and
summons them to your recall with
will of its own. You think you have a
memory; but it has you.
author: N/A

text: Well, let it pass; April is over, April is over. There are all kinds of love in the world, but never the same love twice.
author: N/A
resources: F. Scott Fitzgerald, The Short Stories

text: I am not tragic these days, I don't weep, but I feel alone, bewildered, far from you, far from everything - nothing has any meaning.
author: N/A
resources: Simone de Beauvoir, Letters to Sartre

text:
And I do love you. And you
are awfully interesting. And I
want to protect you from all pains and terrors.
author: N/A

text:
And so I try to be kind
to everything I see, and
in everything I see,
I see him.
author: N/A

text:
All time ever does is pass and
all I ever do is remember.
author: N/A

text: May feels like a mosaic of everything that could have been, but isn't
author: Unknow

text: I adore the texture of your mind.
author: N/A

text:
they want to meet you too,
Every night i tell the stars a story about this lovely person i can't get off my mind.
author: N/A

text: I take a morbid pleasure in complete solitude,
author: Unknow

text: Console me this evening, I am dying of hope.
author: N/A

text: Sab Hai Par Kami Hai Tu
author: N/A

text: In the end, we'll all become stories.
author: N/A

text: Nothing ever ends poetically. It ends and we turn it into poetry. All that blood was never once beautiful. It was just red.
author: Kait Rokowski

text: There were magnets in my bones for that iron in his blood.
author: N/A

text: If I held fate by the throat, I'd make it confess you were never meant to belong to anyone else.
author: N/A

text:
In her eyes, there was an
inquisitive sadness, a sliver of
sky, and a quiet murmur.
author: Unknow

text: And no matter what I still be on your side.
author: N/A

text:
I will write you so deeply in my words
that the one who reads will beg to see you.
author: N/A

text:
my love,
you will never be unloved by me
you are too well tangled into my soul.
author: N/A

text:
Weave me into the fabric of your soul.
Let life be surprised,
When she comes to take one of us
And finds only half.
author: N/A

text:
the year slips away after march.
tomorrow, it will be december.
tomorrow, i will promise not to write about you again.
author: N/A

text:
There is so much stubbornness, so
much rage, and so much ruthlessness
filled in me, yet it amazes me how
easily and how instantly I kneel down
before the one I love.
author: N/A

text: is my love so overwhelming or is it just suffocating?
author: N/A

text:
In the book of my life, your page will
always remain the most special
author: N/A

text:
My eyes met many eyes, but
only got lost in yours.
author: N/A

text:
there is no "in another universe".
It's a nice thought,
but this is it.
This is all we get.
author: N/A

text:
When you held my hand for the first time,
I still remember the feeling of LOVE that you showed,
I've never felt that way before, it was an ethereal feeling
I really wanted to hold you back tight but I couldn't,
Cuz the Ocean of blood was running in my veins, My heart was shattered by my own thoughts n imagination,
But it was your warmth that lead me to the path of eternal peace n happiness,
I felt safe, I felt as if I belong, I matter, I really felt that,
Would you please hold my hand a bit longer, since even a “bit” would feel like eternity
author: N/A

text:
tender is my heart which is ill-fated
to your arms and these red strings
holds the heaviness of your untamable eyes the stare of a thousand stars
oh, love, for I can't look at you but for you, I'd break for you, I'd torn for you, I'd fall
and there is no way out
there is no way out when I'm already home.
author: N/A

text:
Despite the love, i cannot let my suffering make you suffer.
i'd rather cut myself
than let the blade touch your skin.
author: Unknow

text:
The audacity of humans to always walk away
from something they desperately want.
author: Unknow

text:
Strangers become souls,
and moments become memories.
author: N/A

text: Hilarious are the weak who think they are good because they have no claws.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text:
This is where we part,
But know piece of my soul goes with you.
author: N/A

text: Do you believe you could change me, the way I've changed you?
author: N/A

text: The finest souls are those who gulped pain and avoided making others taste it.
author: Nizariat

text:
I tell the Midwest I love him—
his stormy sky yawns forth like the terrible mouth of a beast
author: N/A

text: Only if time was different, if life was a bit fair, everything would be different.
author: N/A

text: One never forgets the taste of certain tears.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: Writing is an intellect's way of bleeding.
author: Unknow

text: Opinion is really the lowest form of human knowledge. It requires no accountability, no understanding. The highest form of knowledge is empathy, for it requires us to suspend our egos and live in another's world. It requires profound purpose larger than the self kind of understanding.
author: Plato

text: If a perfectly moral man entered our world, the people would humiliate him and at last after all manner and suffering they would crucify him.
author: Plato

text: Should I kill myself, or have a cup of coffee? But in the end one needs more courage to live than to kill himself.
author: Albert Camus

text: Plato's theory of soul
author: Plato

text: Man is the only creature who refuses to be what he is.
author: Albert Camus

text: I am the wisest man alive, for I know one thing, and that is that I know nothing.
author: Plato

text: Ignorance, the root and stem of every evil.
author: Plato

text: Do not train a child to learn by force or harshness; but direct them to it by what amuses their minds, so that you may be better able to discover with accuracy the peculiar bent of the genius of each
author: Plato

text: Those who are able to see beyond the shadows and lies of their culture will never be understood, let alone believed, by the masses.
author: Plato

text: Therefore, my son, if you wish to please me, and to bring success and honour to yourself, do right and study, because others will help you if you help yourself
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text:
You really do
get over it one
random day
author: N/A

text: A man so painfully in love is capable of self-torture beyond belief.
author: John Steinbeck

text: Perhaps one did not want to be loved so much as to be understood.
author: George Orwell

text: I never removed anyone from my life, but they all died in the accident of trust.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: Hell is when you must think and feel and do everything that you know you do not want..
author: Carl Jung

text: Know all the theories, master all the techniques, but as you touch a human soul be just another human soul.

text:
I don't want to love
you in a rushed way
I want to learn you.
author: N/A

text:
And as you move on, remember me…
remember us and all we used to be.
author: N/A

text:
time will always pass
everything will age.
youth
It shines, blindingly bright,
for just an instant.
you'l long for that time when it's gone,
it holds the youth of everything you love.
author: N/A

text: I can wait Because you’ll let me in at some point, right?
author: N/A

text:
Your hand is cold, mine
burns like fire. How blind
you are Nastenka.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: There is a certain clinical satisfaction in seeing just how bad things can get.
author: Sylvia Plath
resources: The Unabridged Journals of Sylvia Plath

text:
It's strange how someone can mean
everything to you at one point, and
then become someone you don't
talk to anymore. Not because you stopped
caring, but because life changed, and
you didn't change together
author: Unknow

text:
Sometimes, I do not
want to be kind.
Sometimes, I want to be
vile and push and poke
and prod where I know
it hurts. Sometimes,
there is no kindness left
in me.
author: Unknow

text:
...Our existence
is but a brief
crack of light
between two
eternities of darkness.
author: Vladimir Nabokov

text: There comes a time when you look into the mirror and you realize that what you see is all that you will ever be. And then you accept it. Or you kill yourself. Or you stop looking in mirrors.
author: J. Michael Straczynski
resources: Babylon 5: The Scripts of J. Michael Straczynski, Vol. 2

text: Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart. The really great men must, I think, have great sadness on earth.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: As For Me, All I Know Is That I Know Nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Wisdom begins in wonder.
author: Socrates

text: But than it passed, as all things do.
author: Unknow

text:
Don't explain your philosophy.
Embody it.
author: Epictetus

text:
The purpose of knowledge
is action, not knowledge
author: Aristotle

text: It is better to be unhappy and know the worst, than to be happy in a fool's paradise.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: It's foolish to desire what you refuse to suffer for.
author: Unknow

text:
Today the world feels gentler because you arrived in it. May this new year hold you softly, return your kindness to you in quiet ways,
and remind you again and you-again again-that your existence is a gift time itself celebrates
author: N/A

please do not fall
in love with me
for I am devotion
wrapped in doubt

in expectation of the night,
and the night
in fear of the dawn.

text:
What I would not give to forget thing or two.
Memory gives moments immortality, but forgetfulness promotes a healthy mind. It’s good to forget

text:
Maybe You weren't as beautiful as I believed, nor as precious as l imagined.
Maybe it was never You who was flawless, but my eyes that dressed You in perfection.
And now, as the illusion fades, I wonder if I ever loved You-or just the picture I painted of You in my mind.
author: Unknow

text:
What is strength?
To be able to create miracles?
To be able to overcome misery & pain?
To be able to regain lost glory?
Or all of these?
author: Rushi

text: To be trusted is a greater compliment than being loved.
author: George MacDonald

text: According to psychology, the degree to which a person can grow is directly proportional to the amount of truth they can accept-about themselves, without running away.
author: Leland Val Van De Wall

text: Emperor Fushimi‘says
author: Emperor Fushimi

text: Yea, sure coffee never betrays, and solitude never demands— but now I catch myself wishing you were there, as if solitude itself had learned your name, and coffee has started to taste more like you
author: Rushi

text: This too shall pass.

text: Thinking of you is a poison I drink often.

author: N/A
